edge_hypothesis: The RSI pullback ACCEPT (PF=1.21, DD=9.2%, 38 trades) is fragile due to low trade count. The recent rp63a8e1 spec tests 1h with relaxed RSI — this spec takes a fundamentally different approach: SHORTER trend EMA on 4h with extreme R:R. The current rsi_pullback uses ema_trend=200 (EMA_200), which is extremely slow-moving on 4h (each bar = 4 hours, so EMA_200 = 800 hours of smoothing). By shortening to ema_trend=100 (EMA_100), the trend filter adapts ~2x faster to regime changes. This means: (1) trend reversals are recognized sooner, generating earlier entry signals; (2) more bars qualify as 'above trend' because EMA_100 tracks price more closely than EMA_200; (3) combined effect: more trade signals from the same timeframe without relaxing RSI quality. The RSI threshold stays at 40 (the proven ACCEPT level) — we're NOT diluting signal quality, we're increasing signal QUANTITY by using a faster trend reference. Combined with extreme R:R (5:1 to 7:1), this tests whether RSI pullback entries in faster-recognized trends catch large continuation moves. The pullback logic is sound: when RSI dips to 40 in a confirmed uptrend, it represents temporary selling pressure in a continuing trend — a 'buy the dip' signal. With a faster trend EMA, these dips are recognized in younger trends that still have more upside left to capture. Invalidation: (1) if PF < 1.0 at ema_trend=100, the shorter EMA produces too many false trend signals — trend identification needs the slow EMA_200; (2) if trade count doesn't increase meaningfully vs the 38-trade ACCEPT, the bottleneck is RSI reaching 40 (not the trend gate).

variants.name: rsi_pullback_fast_trend_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with EMA_100 trend filter (instead of default EMA_200) and 5:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 7.5x TP). The faster trend EMA adapts ~2x quicker to regime changes, producing earlier entry signals in younger trends with more upside remaining. RSI threshold stays at 40 (the proven ACCEPT level). EMA_50 slope confirmation provides momentum validation. At 5:1 R:R with breakeven win rate of 17%, this is achievable if pullback entries in younger trends produce continuation moves of 7.5x ATR.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 confirms uptrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 40 indicates pullback within uptrend (proven ACCEPT threshold).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 confirms downtrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 17% win rate to break even after fees.
Tests faster trend recognition with extreme R:R continuation capture
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend filter (2x faster than default EMA_200) + EMA_50 slope confirmation
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback in uptrend (proven ACCEPT threshold, unchanged)
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — 5:1 R:R for trend continuation capture

variants.name: rsi_pullback_fast_trend_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with EMA_100 trend filter and extreme 7:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 10.5x TP). The most aggressive variant — tests whether pullback entries in younger trends can catch massive continuation moves. On ETH/4h, 10.5x ATR = $525-840 of price movement, corresponding to multi-day trend legs. The hypothesis: RSI=40 in a young trend (EMA_100) means the trend has just started and the pullback creates a high-quality entry for the full trend leg. At breakeven win rate of 12.5%, even a low hit rate is viable.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 confirms uptrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 40 indicates pullback within uptrend (proven ACCEPT threshold).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 confirms downtrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
Maximum R:R variant: tests whether young-trend pullbacks catch full trend legs
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend filter (faster trend recognition) + EMA_50 slope
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback — extreme R:R tests full trend leg capture from young trends
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — 7:1 R:R maximum variant

variants.name: rsi_pullback_fast_trend_relaxed
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with EMA_100 trend filter, RELAXED RSI (rsi_long_max=45), and 4:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 6.0x TP). Signal quantity variant: both faster trend EMA AND relaxed RSI should maximize trade count. The hypothesis: EMA_100 + RSI<=45 captures more pullback events while still maintaining trend-quality entries. The moderate 4:1 R:R is a balance — enough R:R for fee drag elimination but not so extreme that win rate drops too low. If this variant produces 60+ trades with PF > 1.0, the RSI pullback edge is validated as robust (not dependent on narrow parameter choices).
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 confirms uptrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 45 indicates pullback within uptrend (relaxed for more signals).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 confirms downtrend (shorter trend filter for faster adaptation).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 >= 55 indicates pullback within downtrend (relaxed for more signals).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 4:1. Needs 20% win rate to break even after fees.
Quantity variant: tests whether wider signal net maintains edge with moderate R:R
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 faster trend + EMA_50 slope — maximizes signal quantity
RSI_14 <= 45 relaxed pullback — wider net for more trades to validate edge robustness
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 6.0x TP — moderate 4:1 R:R balance
